CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 18078038) FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT INTERLINE BRANDS, INC. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 7.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT INTERLINE BRANDS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF LISA FULTON AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. CROSS-DEFENDANT INTERLINE BRANDS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF LISA FULTON IS GRANTED AND ITS REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. MS. FULTON'S DEPOSITION MUST OCCUR ON OR BEFORE OCTOBER 26, 2018. THE PRIMARY GROUND ASSERTED TO OPPOSE THIS MOTION IS THAT ALL INFORMATION KNOWN BY MS. FULTON RELATING TO THIS CASE IS COVERED BY THE WORK PRODUCT PRIVILEGE OF PLAINTIFF STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY. HOWEVER, STATE FARM HAS NOT SUBMITTED ANY EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT MS. FULTON WAS RETAINED BY OR ON BEHALF OF ANY ATTORNEY, SO STATE FARM HAS FAILED TO ESTABLISH THE FOUNDATION FOR WORK PRODUCT. WHILE IT MAY BE THAT MS. FULTON HAS LITTLE OR NOTHING PERTINENT TO SAY ABOUT STATE FARM'S SUBROGATION CLAIM, THAT IS NOT A BASIS TO PRECLUDE HER DEPOSITION, ESPECIALLY GIVEN THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINTS. IN ALL EVENTS, INSTEAD OF ADVISING MS. FULTON NOT TO ATTEND HER DEPOSITION WHICH IS WHAT APPEARS THAT STATE FARM DID, THE PROPER COURSE WAS FOR STATE FARM TO FILE A MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER. BECAUSE THERE APPEARS TO BE SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR STATE FARM'S POSITIONS, NO MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED.COUNSEL FOR INTERLINE TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-12-2019 10:30 AM FOR DEPT 610

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dave Marcolini, Esq. specially apperaing for Cross Defendant Interline Brands.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Cross Defendant Interline Brands, Inc.'s Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Lisa Fulton and Request For Sanctions.

Cross-defendant Interline Brands, Inc.'s motion to compel the deposition of Lisa Fulton is granted and its request for monetary sanctions is denied. Ms. Fulton's deposition must occur on or before October 26, 2018. The primary ground asserted to oppose this motion is that all information known by Ms. Fulton relating to this case is covered by the work product privilege of plaintiff State Farm General Insurance Company. However, State Farm has not submitted any evidence showing that Ms. Fulton was retained by or on behalf of any attorney, so State Farm has failed to establish the foundation for work product. While it may be that Ms. Fulton has little or nothing pertinent to say about State Farm's subrogation claim, that is not a basis to preclude her deposition, especially given the allegations of the cross-complaints. In all events, instead of advising Ms. Fulton not to attend her deposition which is what appears that State Farm did, the proper course was for State Farm to file a motion for protective order. Because there appears to be substantial justification for State Farm's positions, no monetary sanctions are awarded.

Counsel for Interline to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ashley Stander of Law Offices of Geordan Goebal telephone number 805-482-7966 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff State Farm General Insurance.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Robert Lord's demurrer to the second cause of action in the complaint for trespass is overruled. Liberally construed, the complaint adequately alleges a claim for trespass against Mr. Lord. "Trespass is an unlawful interference with possession of property' Liability for trespass may be imposed for conduct which is intentional, reckless, negligent or the result of an extrahazardous activity." (Staples v. Hoefke (1987) 189 Cal.App.3d 1397, 1406; see also CACI 2000.) Paragraph 5 of the complaint alleges that the defendants are agents of each other and paragraphs 9, 15, and 16 allege that the defendants (including Mr. Masias and Mr. Lord) caused water to intrude onto Ms. Perri's premises from Mr. Lord's premises. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)
